steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "the observatory sites…number nearly five ______ across the globe, the radars track ionospheric irregularities and other geospace phenomena." After the blank, a comma joins what appears to be a second independent clause ("the radars track…"). That comma creates a comma splice unless the blank itself provides a proper sentence boundary.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — dozen. Located: ends the first sentence cleanly with a period after "dozen," then begins a new sentence "Located across the globe, the radars track…" — a correct participial opener. ✓
  • Choice B — dozen, located: creates a comma splice between two independent clauses ("sites…number nearly five dozen" and "the radars track…"). ✗
  • Choice C — dozen, which are located: turns the second clause into a relative clause modifying "dozen," so the main verb "track" has no subject — a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — dozen located: leaves the comma after the blank still splicing two independent clauses. ✗
That is the correct answer.
